title: Legacy001 CatBoost GPU second expert
summary: Add CatBoost GPU as a single second expert alongside the legacy-001 RealMLP baseline and compare or average their OOF probabilities.
rationale: CatBoost can capture categorical interactions and threshold effects differently from RealMLP. Testing it as the first distinct model family gives a cleaner signal than immediately adding multiple GBDTs and a stacker.
implementation_hint: Use the same folds as the parent. Train CatBoost with task_type='GPU' and devices='0' when GPU is enabled, generate fold OOF and test probabilities, report RealMLP OOF AUC, CatBoost OOF AUC, their prediction correlation, and a simple average. Do not add logistic stacking, calibration, XGBoost, or LightGBM in this patch.
expected_effect: Improve ensemble AUC if CatBoost errors are not perfectly correlated with RealMLP, even if CatBoost standalone is slightly weaker.
risk: CatBoost may underperform or require careful categorical handling; GPU memory/runtime can become significant.
